channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump nominates Marty Makary, a critic of some COVID-19 health measures, to lead the FDA
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday nominated Dr. Marty Makary to lead the Food and Drug Administration, selecting a surgeon and author who gained national attention for opposing vaccine mandates and some other public health measures during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Project 2025 author Russell Vought to lead budget office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump tapped Project 2025 co-author Russell Vought to serve as the director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) during his second administration. Vought served as Trump's OMB deputy director during his first term in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: California entrepreneur who led minimum wage measure 'disappointed' by defeat, vows to fight for working class
item.description: The backer of California's recently defeated minimum wage measure said he was "disappointed" but vowed to keep advocating for higher wages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance takes on a more visible transition role, working to boost Trump's most contentious picks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After several weeks working mostly behind closed doors, Vice President-elect JD Vance returned to Capitol Hill this week in a new, more visible role: Helping Donald Trump try to get his most contentious Cabinet picks to confirmation in the Senate, where Vance has served for the las
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matt Gaetz says he won't return to Congress next year after withdrawing name for attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Rep. Matt Gaetz said Friday that he will not be returning to Congress after withdrawing his name from consideration to be attorney general under President-elect Donald Trump amid growing allegations of sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge reduces prison sentence for Capitol rioter who berated and insulted him
item.description: A federal judge has imposed a one-year reduction in a prison sentence for a man who stormed the U.S. Capitol and then engaged in a pattern of disruptive courtroom behavior, including berating and insulting the judge.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump names former Florida AG Pam Bondi as his new pick for U.S. attorney general
item.description: After former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration, President-elect Donald Trump named Pam Bondi, a former attorney general of Florida, as his next pick for U.S. attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Incumbent Iowa Republican says Dem House opponent 'wasting taxpayer money' with recount
item.description: Republican Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks has called a Democrat-backed recount of her 2024 House race a waste of taxpayer money while she leads Christina Bohannan by fewer than 1,000 votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans open to RFK Jr. at HHS despite abortion-rights stance
item.description: Senate Republicans are signaling that they are open to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination to lead the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) despite his long-standing support of abortion rights and concerns emanating from anti-abortion voices about his selection.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump picks Pam Bondi for next attorney general
item.description: President-elect Trump has named former Florida Attorney General Pam Bondi as his next nominee to lead the Department of Justice (DOJ). Bondi was a swift pick just hours after former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew himself from consideration for the role.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Donald Trump Cabinet will have zero policy input
item.description: Bill O'Reilly tells NewsNation's "CUOMO" that Trump just wants people in his Cabinet that will do exactly as he tells them to do.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Dave McCormick reacts to Dr. Oz's nomination to lead Medicare, Medicaid
item.description: "The problem, doctor, is there's no miracle cure for flip-flopping," McCormick said while standing next to Oz on the debate stage.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Trump selects Matthew Whitaker as NATO ambassador
item.description: In a statement, Trump said Whitaker is a "strong warrior and loyal Patriot, who will ensure the United States' interests are advanced and defended.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Matthew Whitaker for ambassador to NATO
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump tapped Matthew Whitaker as his ambassador to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization, which Trump has long criticized for taking advantage of U.S. defense spending.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas and Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Trump's immigration grand strategy casts wide net
item.description: President-elect Trump is returning to the White House looking to dwarf his first term's significant impact on immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks business executive Linda McMahon to lead the Education Department
item.description: McMahon is a professional wrestling business magnate and co-chair of President-elect Donald Trump's transition team. She has limited experience working with K-12 public schools.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Nevada attorney general pledges to combat 'unconstitutional mandates' amid Trump election
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford said his office was prepared to address any “fear and uncertainty” following the election of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State Board of Education approves Bible-infused curriculum
item.description: Critics say the curriculum overemphasizes Christianity. Texas school districts don’t have to use it but will receive $60 per student if they do.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Manhattan prosecutors ask for additional pause in Trump hush-money criminal case
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors are asking for a further pause in the criminal trial against President-elect Donald Trump in order to give both sides time to weigh the unprecedented nature of the situation.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson becomes 'MAGA' Mike, cementing place in Trump's circle
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is cementing his place in President-elect Trump’s inner orbit, becoming not only a visible — if slightly out-of-place-looking — part of his entourage, but a key supporter of his no-holds-barred approach to his second term.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Top pollster Ann Selzer is set to retire from the Des Moines Register’s Iowa Poll after a shocking presidential survey was so wrong.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg called to apologize for saying bakery denied order over politics
item.description: New York City politicians are calling on Whoopi Goldberg to apologize for suggesting on air that a local bakery had declined to make “The View” co-host a batch of desserts for her birthday because of her political beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, WFLA, NBC
